mentions.note: Up only 13% in 2025, rolled over after Q3 earnings. Capex 2025 $70-72B, rising to maybe $100B in 2026. No clear AI strategy beyond ads; lacks leading generative AI platform or cloud business. Cramer likes Zuckerberg but wants clarity. Power gating (supply constraints) may limit actual spending. Big position in Charitable Trust.

Buying nuclear power and committing to small modular reactors (SMRs) from Oklo for 2030 delivery sound great but historically nuclear projects face endemic delays and cost overruns. 2030 timeline unrealistic; worker and material shortages evident. Be cautious on execution.
Social media strength visible in Taiwan Semi transcript; benefiting from hyperscaler AI capex validation and demand confirmation.
Down 8% YTD; mega-cap tech underperformance vs. speculative names suggests rotation away from quality.
P/E multiple contracted from 30x to 22x earnings; market skeptical of growth rate; Cramer says Meta has 'lost its way' amid OpenAI competition and generative AI platform threats.
Terrible until today; nice move yesterday, recommended last 2 days. Mark Zuckerberg lowered boom on spending all capex can; explain how making money on data center buildout beyond smart glasses.

Trading <23x earnings after 16% decline from August highs. Strong ad numbers won't matter if 2026 CapEx guidance disappoints again. Catalyst needed on CapEx or AI ROI clarity. Watch Wednesday earnings.
Massive earnings and revenue beat; confident can grow operating income YoY despite $135B AI spend; rallying furiously.
AI-powered advertising with 3.5B daily users now proven force multiplier; Mark Zuckerberg confirmed AI is greatest competitive advantage; not done going higher.
Terrific quarter but all market says is 'just ad company with Ray-Bans.' Zero effort to defend or promote stock; hard to maintain enthusiasm.

Beat top and bottom; Zuckerberg explained AI boosting ad profitability; $115–135B capex acceptable; guidance excellent; stock rallied 10% then slid 5-of-6 sessions, erasing gains—buy on free quarter; own for Charitable Trust.

Caller Robert asks about volatility (up/down each quarter). Cramer speculates Zuckerberg could embrace AI-driven workforce cut (Block-style) to unlock shareholder value; conjecture but plausible catalyst.

Lost court cases on social-media-as-tobacco theory; stock lost tens of billions interim but earnings ultimately unimpaired. Pattern: interim pain, then appellate reversal, then recovery (cf. J&J). Hold through vindication but respect timing lag.

Two state-court losses ($375M New Mexico, $6M California addictive social media) look existential but appellate precedent (J&J talc) shows federal courts overturn; free speech defense strong. Selloff excessive; buyers should step in.
Trading at 19x earnings after New Mexico and Los Angeles court losses; cooler heads recognize appeals ahead, First Amendment protection likely, awards will be trimmed.
Announced new AI model (Muse Spark); surged 4.4% on top of prior day gains; crisis-narrative concerns overblown; Cramer sees no actual crisis.
Major Nvidia customer despite developing own chips; both Meta and Amazon remain huge Nvidia buyers; perception of chip independence overstated.
10% layoff announcement; don't panic. Zuckerberg ('chainsaw') cuts when unnecessary—ruthless cost control. Stock should go higher, not lower.
Reports Wednesday; hyperscaler capex test. Watch for guidance on AI data center spending.
Big Four earnings tomorrow (Wed); got clobbered Oct 29 2025; watch capex guidance.
Raised capex by $10B; that's a red flag. While revenue up 33% (fastest in 5 years), Cramer needs more justification for spend before reversing to buy.

33% revenue growth (best in 5 years), 62% EPS growth, but family DAP shrank vs prior quarter, CapEx raised $10B to $125–145B for component inflation; lacks cloud infrastructure monetization path like peers; investors unconvinced on AI spending justification; stock down 8%.
33% YoY growth (fastest 5 years), 3.5B daily active users, $150B capex approaching; market dismissing growth for founder behavior—time to stop worrying and start buying.

Magnificent Seven member owned by Cramer's charitable trust. Original FANG member (Facebook); digital advertising dominance; resilient earnings growth despite antitrust scrutiny; rate-proof fundamentals.
Important AI capabilities; mentioned as context for tech dominance but not specifically recommended for action.
Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, AI, eyeglasses comprise powerful portfolio yet not 'printing money' like before. Unclear what happens next; hasn't excelled in visible way lately.

Leading AR/VR wearables race with best-in-class hardware. Meta's programs teaching small/medium businesses trades (electricians, plumbers, data center technicians) demonstrate smart capital deployment for future workforce development; can't salute them enough.
